title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 9 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England has mandated four joint lead managers (RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley) for an issuance operation to finance its foreign exchange reserves via a benchmark 3-year US dollar bond. This marks the first 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme announced in September 2025, which shifted to two benchmark issuances annually with a regular timetable and high transparency.
key_points: The Bank of England has mandated RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley as Joint Lead Managers for the issuance
The operation will be a benchmark 3-year US dollar bond
Execution is expected in the near future, subject to market conditions
This is the first 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme pursuant to the Market Notice published on 23 September 2025
The September 2025 announcement established a framework of two benchmark issuances annually with regular timetable and high transparency
The Bank holds foreign exchange reserves separately from the Government's own foreign exchange reserves
why_it_matters: This notice documents the operational mechanics of the BoE's foreign exchange reserve financing programme. The use of four major banks as joint lead managers for a USD-denominated benchmark issuance affects USD bond market dynamics and signals the BoE's commitment to transparent, regular reserve financing operations.
market_structure_context: The source describes the BoE's updated foreign currency reserves issuance programme, including the regular benchmark issuance framework (two per year) established in September 2025 and the selection of joint lead managers for the 3-year USD bond operation.
notes: The source is a factual market notice documenting the BoE's reserve financing operation. Content is relevant to market structure as it describes central bank issuance operations and the use of primary dealers. The notice contains standard legal disclaimers and forward-looking statement caveats but the core operational information is clear. Note that much of the source text consists of jurisdictional restrictions and legal notices rather than substantive market content.
